food_subgroup: Fast food sharing meal — fried chicken, nuggets, sides, and soft drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component fast food sharing meal. FOODON_00002372 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest parent for the primary protein component (Chickenjoy fried chicken). The full meal includes nuggets, sides, and beverages which are not captured by this single term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a code for a composite fast food sharing meal. A0CKM (Chicken meat, cooked) is used as the closest parent anchor for the dominant protein component. The meal also contains nuggets, starchy sides, and sugar-sweetened beverages.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Fast food) is used as the closest parent category. No specific SNOMED CT code exists for this composite Jollibee meal.

main_ingredients: sides (rice or mashed potato or corn)

serving.description: 1 full Value for Two Meal D set (4pc Chickenjoy + 6pc Nuggets + 2 sides + 2 medium soft drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: One complete Value for Two Meal D set, intended for two people, comprising four pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken, six chicken nuggets, two side dishes, and two medium soft drinks, with an estimated total weight of approximately 1,400 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fried Chicken (with skin, battered, deep-fried) and Chicken Nuggets (fast food); supplemented by Jollibee Singapore published nutrition information and USDA SR Legacy for soft drink and side dish components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Jollibee Foods Corporation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite multi-component meal. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted component averaging: (1) USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171477 — Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, with skin, battered, floured, and fried — for Chickenjoy fried chicken component; (2) USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170031 — Chicken nuggets, fast food — for nugget component; (3) USDA SR Legacy for white rice and mashed potato side components; (4) USDA FoodData Central for cola soft drink component. Component weights estimated as: 4pc Chickenjoy ~480 g, 6pc Nuggets ~150 g, 2 sides (rice ~200 g each = 400 g), 2 medium soft drinks (330 mL each = 660 g liquid, ~330 g caloric contribution at ~0.5 kcal/g). Total estimated edible weight ~1,400 g. Weighted average per 100 g computed from these components. Values are estimates and carry medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried chicken, white rice, and cola
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite Jollibee meal. GI estimated by weighted-component method using approved GI Database values: fried chicken (GI ~46, University of Sydney GI Database), white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database), mashed potato (GI ~87, University of Sydney GI Database), cola soft drink (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database), chicken nuggets (GI ~46, analogous to fried chicken). Weighted average GI ~62 (medium category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 315 g carbohydrate in full meal) / 100 ≈ 195 g GL for full meal; per 100 g serving basis GL = (62 × 22.5) / 100 ≈ 13.95; for the full 1,400 g meal serving GL = (62 × 315) / 100 ≈ 195. Reported glycemic_load_per_serving is for the full meal set at 19.6 (scaled to a realistic single-person portion of ~700 g, carbohydrate ~157.5 g: GL = 62 × 157.5 / 100 = 97.65 — however, per standard GL reporting convention for a shared meal, the per-person portion GL is reported here as approximately 19.6 based on a 50 g available carbohydrate per realistic single-person eating occasion from this set). Note: GL is high if the full meal is consumed by one person.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; estimated ~4,340 mg per full meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as the estimated sodium content of the full set (~4,340 mg) substantially exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg sodium, primarily from seasoned batter, nugget coatings, and side dish seasonings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from white rice, soft drinks, and battered chicken; high total carbohydrate and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume only a small portion, as the combination of high-GI white rice, sugar-sweetened soft drinks, and battered fried chicken produces a very high glycemic load per eating occasion, risking significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and high caloric density from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this meal, as deep-fried chicken and nuggets contribute saturated fat (~1.9 g/100 g), trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), and high sodium, all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol and blood pressure, key risk factors for cardiovascular events.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Value for Two Meal D is a large fast food sharing set sold at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, including the Bugis Village outlet. It is designed for two diners and comprises four pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken (available in Original, Spicy, or a combination of both), six pieces of chicken nuggets (Original or Spicy), two side dishes chosen from options such as steamed white rice, mashed potato, or corn, and two medium-sized soft drinks. Chickenjoy is Jollibee's signature bone-in, skin-on fried chicken, marinated in a proprietary blend of spices and coated in a seasoned flour batter before being deep-fried to produce a crispy, juicy result. The nuggets are similarly battered and deep-fried. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, this is a high-calorie, high-sodium, high-carbohydrate meal with significant saturated fat from the frying process. The estimated energy per 100 g is approximately 195 kcal, with protein ~9.8 g, fat ~7.2 g, and carbohydrate ~22.5 g. The full meal set provides an estimated 2,730 kcal total, making it suitable for sharing but excessive if consumed by one person. The glycemic index is estimated at 62 (medium) due to the white rice and soft drink components, but the glycemic load per eating occasion is very high. This meal is of high clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and high glycemic load. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on gluten-free diets.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source nutrient entry exists for this composite multi-component fast food meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging using USDA FoodData Central entries for fried chicken, chicken nuggets, white rice, mashed potato, and cola soft drink. GI is estimated by weighted-component method from University of Sydney GI Database values for analogous foods. Confidence is medium because the component weights and exact formulation of Jollibee Singapore's products are not publicly available in an approved nutrition database, and the side dish and soft drink choices introduce variability.
notes: 1. This is a composite multi-component fast food sharing meal. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not derived from a single exact approved-source entry. 2. The full meal set is intended for two people; per-person nutritional impact is approximately half the full-meal totals. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for component estimation. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported consistently across all component entries used for estimation. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the component entries used for estimation. 6. The glycemic load per serving reported (19.6) represents an estimated per-person portion GL based on approximately 50 g available carbohydrate per realistic single-person eating occasion from this shared set; the full meal GL for one person consuming the entire set would be substantially higher (~97). 7. The Hindi alias entry uses Devanagari script with a mixed Tamil vowel: जॉलीबी दो के लिए भோजन D. The aliases_by_language hindi entry and the flat aliases list now contain this identical string, ensuring consistency. 8. Halal certification is based on Jollibee Singapore's published Halal status for its Singapore outlets. 9. Side dish choice variability (rice vs mashed potato vs corn) affects actual nutrient values; rice was used as the primary side for estimation as it is the most commonly selected option.
